topic: EXP-211-redesign v2 — accuracy-math demotion v2 redraft (FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE / Phase A2 / EXP-A6)
our_argument_summary: v2 redraft of EXP-211 per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001. Three coordinated edits to build-scripts/generate-html.js: (a) strip 'Honest accuracy: X, not Y' arithmetic clause from the hero 4+4 'Actual Failures' sc-card sublabel; (b) delete the bordered accent box (lines 1386–1388) containing the headline-is-hardcoded prose; (c) insert a new <section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading"> with visible <h3> consolidating the moved arithmetic AND the source-code critique; (d) add CSS rules. v2 supersedes v1 (DRAFT_AWAITING) and is routed under the conditional-approval rubric — clean re-review (no new ESCALATE) → automatic integration. v2's stated remit: fix three operator-flagged regressions (bare <aside> landmark-skip a11y, one-scroll reachability erosion, missing --ink-3 contrast statement) plus four curmudgeon minor edits (H1 ternary inconsistency, H2 'verbatim' overreach, H3 kicker phrasing, H4 CC2 narrative reachability).

relevant_files: build-scripts/generate-html.js (lines 1365–1390 — Overview hero + bordered box + Downloads heading)
build-scripts/generate-html.js (lines 517–532 — existing .scorecard/.sc-card CSS rules; v2 adds .accuracy-math-section rules near here)
package.json (playwright ^1.59.1 is a dependency, used by build-scripts/generate-pdf.js)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: v2 is a pure presentation edit — no data pipeline change. All numeric values inside the new section are derived from existing generate-html.js context variables (${counts.total}, ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}, ${silentFailures}, ${honestAccuracy}, ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}, ${accuracyVariantList}). No new computation introduced. Build reproducibility (NC6) preserved.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: All section numerics are template-substituted from generate-html.js context. No hardcoded numbers in the section. NC5 (computed-counts rule) honored.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: v2 hedges acceptance criteria where measurement requires Playwright integration that test.js does not currently have (AC8/9/10/12). notes_for_human_review_group[2] flags this and offers a downgrade path to static layout assertions. Acceptable but requires integration-plan decision.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies:
One minor line-number documentation discrepancy: change_3.where states 'currently line 1385 closing ' for the sc-hero closing tag, but the sc-hero's closing is actually at the start of line 1386 (sharing line 1386 with the opening of the bordered accent box: '
'). Line 1385 is the closing
of the inner Timestamp Error sc-card. The structural anchor (after sc-hero close, before bordered box) is unambiguous from context, but the line number is one off.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — this is a project-internal HTML/CSS spec, not a dome-model claim review.

H1_defensive_ternary_inline.v1_finding: change_3.new_aside_html showed the unconditional ams-variant paragraph; the ternary wrap was buried in anticipated_objections[5]. A decider integrating from the new_aside_html template would ship the unwrapped version and introduce a double-space grammar bug when variantSources is empty.
H1_defensive_ternary_inline.v2_fix:
VERIFIED FIXED. change_3.new_section_html now contains the inline ternary: ${accuracyVariantList ? `

…

` : ''}. AC6 enforces this with a regression test (HTML must NOT contain the substring 'Internal queries return depending' with double space, and must NOT contain an empty

). The integration target is now self-consistent.

H2_verbatim_overreach.v1_finding: content_preservation_audit claimed source-code-critique clauses preserved 'verbatim' but introduced word-level changes ('HTML source code' → 'HTML source'; 'When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns X' → 'Internal queries return X'). The word 'verbatim' overstated the preservation.
H2_verbatim_overreach.v2_fix: PARTIALLY FIXED. Top-level preservation_framing_note explicitly downgrades to 'near-verbatim (minor wording polish, no clause dropped)'. Individual clause descriptions still use mixed terminology — 'near-verbatim', 'verbatim', 'verbatim modulo punctuation', 'VERBATIM' — but each per-clause use is locally accurate (the operator-mandated 'self-serving denominator' sentence IS verbatim; the 'No Python script…' sentence IS verbatim). The aggregate framing note covers the audit's overall claim. See H5 for a residual nit.
H2_verbatim_overreach.assessment: Substantively addressed. Residual phrasing-consistency nit (see H5).

H3_kicker_phrasing.v1_finding: The kicker text 'Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader' editorializes about the audience. Recommendation: adopt the analyst's second-choice default 'Accuracy math — supporting detail.'
H3_kicker_phrasing.v2_fix:
VERIFIED FIXED. change_3.new_section_html sets

Accuracy math — supporting detail

. AC20 asserts this exact string. notes_for_human_review_group[4] documents the choice.

H4_CC2_narrative_reachability.v1_finding: CC2 'one-scroll reachability from hero card' was a narrative claim with a squishy 'two scrolls if the bar chart pushes the aside' fallback. Recommendation: add concrete Playwright DOM.getBoundingClientRect() measurement at integration time with explicit scrollY targets for 1080p, 1440p, and 390×844 mobile.
H4_CC2_narrative_reachability.v2_fix: VERIFIED FIXED in spec. AC8 (1920×1080), AC9 (2560×1440), AC10 (390×844 mobile) state explicit scrollY thresholds. AC8: heading top <= 1080, first arithmetic line top <= 1512. AC9: heading + first line top <= 1440 (full above-fold). AC10: heading top <= 1266, single-column ams-grid verified. These are testable assertions, not narrative claims. See H3 for residual threshold-strictness nit and H6 for test-infrastructure gap.
H4_CC2_narrative_reachability.assessment: Substantively addressed. Two follow-on minor concerns (H3, H6 below) about how the assertions are written/run.

finding_1_self_serving_sentence.operator_concern: v1 originally dropped the 'self-serving denominator' sentence. ALREADY RESOLVED by HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 in v1.
finding_1_self_serving_sentence.v2_status: Sentence preserved verbatim in v2 in the same structural location (source-code-critique paragraph, after the negative enumeration, before the Section 6.6 link). Verified by reading change_3.new_section_html directly: 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' is present, exact wording. Operator override honored.

finding_2_aside_landmark_skip.operator_concern: v1 used <aside class="accuracy-math-aside">. <aside> defaults to ARIA role=complementary, which screen-readers offer as a SKIPPABLE landmark in landmark-shortcut navigation. For a load-bearing critique, skippable is wrong.
finding_2_aside_landmark_skip.v2_fix:
Element changed to
with a visible

Accuracy math — supporting detail

. Two independent a11y pathways now exist: (a) named landmark navigation (region with accessible name from h3), (b) heading navigation (the

enters the document outline, reachable via H key in NVDA/JAWS or headings rotor in VoiceOver). AC4, AC5, AC12, AC13 are testable assertions.
finding_2_aside_landmark_skip.assessment: Sound fix. Caveat: the semantic improvement is real but moderate — both <aside role=complementary> AND <section role=region> are landmarks reachable via landmark navigation; the more substantive a11y gain is the new heading-navigation pathway from the visible <h3>. v2's framing of <aside> as 'silently skippable' is a defensible interpretation but slightly oversells the magnitude of the landmark-vs-region distinction. Net: the fix is correct and the new heading pathway is a genuine win.

finding_3_one_scroll_reachability.operator_concern: v1's narrative reachability claim ('1-2 scrolls') was untestable without measurement. Promote to concrete viewport check in acceptance_criteria.
finding_3_one_scroll_reachability.v2_fix: AC8/9/10 state explicit Playwright-measured scrollY thresholds at three viewport sizes. v2 also documents the placement at 'after sc-hero close, before Downloads heading' — the same physical position v1 already occupied, but documented correctly (v1 referenced 'sc-breakdown' which doesn't exist as an Overview-tab structural element).
finding_3_one_scroll_reachability.assessment: Substantively addressed at the spec level. See H6 for the test-infrastructure gap that affects how AC8/9/10 actually get measured.

finding_additional_ink3_contrast.operator_concern: Additional requirement: state WCAG contrast ratios for --ink-3 in light and dark themes.
finding_additional_ink3_contrast.v2_fix: AC14 states measured ratios for both --ink-2 and --ink-3 over --bg in both themes. All four pass AA body 4.5:1.
finding_additional_ink3_contrast.assessment: Clean fix. INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED — see contrast_verification below.

contrast_verification.method: Recomputed AC14's stated ratios using the WCAG 2.1 relative-luminance formula (sRGB → linear via the piecewise transform; L = 0.2126*R + 0.7152*G + 0.0722*B; contrast = (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05)).
contrast_verification.tokens_verified_against_source: Read --ink-2/--ink-3/--bg directly from build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 496 (light :root), 497 (dark @media prefers-color-scheme), and 645 (print). Token values match AC14 exactly: --ink-2 dark #b8b5ad / light #3A352D; --ink-3 dark #8a8680 / light #6E675B; --bg dark #1a1a2e / light #fff.
contrast_verification.note: All four AC14 ratios are independently verified to two decimal places. v2 transparently flags the --ink-3 dark tightness (4.71:1 vs 4.5:1 threshold) in notes_for_human_review_group[3]. A future EXP-202 revision that shifts --ink-3 darker would need re-measurement; this risk is documented.

line_anchors_verified.line 1379 (hero sublabel for 'Actual Fai:
VERIFIED. Line 1379 is the
He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Honest accuracy: ${honestAccuracy}, not ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}. Details →
— matches change_1.current_sublabel_html.
line_anchors_verified.lines 1386–1388 (bordered accent box):
VERIFIED. Line 1386 contains '
' (sc-hero close + bordered box open on the same line). Line 1387 is the prose

. Line 1388 is the closing

. The bordered box spans lines 1386–1388 inclusive — matches change_2.where.
line_anchors_verified.line 1385 closing </div> attribution to: INCORRECT. Line 1385 is the closing of the inner Timestamp Error sc-card (lines 1381–1385). The sc-hero's closing is the FIRST at the start of line 1386 (which then continues with the bordered box opening). v2's change_3.where mis-attributes line 1385 as the sc-hero close. See H1 below.
line_anchors_verified.~line 520 area for new CSS rules: VERIFIED. Existing .scorecard/.sc-card rules are at lines 517–532. v2's stated insertion point (~line 520) is accurate.

generate_html_layout_verification.overview_structural_order_post_integrati:
VERIFIED matches AC18 with one omission: AC18 lists 'Downloads heading → verdict-legend' but skips the actual

Verdict Categories Used in This Review

on line 1396 between Downloads and the legend. Non-essential omission (the

sits between AC18's 'Downloads' and 'verdict-legend' nodes; not a structural defect).

content_preservation_diff_analysis.summary: v2 audit's 'near-verbatim' framing is substantively correct, but the lead-clause word loss is not itemized.

clause_by_clause.original: The headline "X" accuracy is not computed by any script in the model's repository.
clause_by_clause.v2: The headline 'X' is not computed by any script in the model's repository.
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Drops the word 'accuracy' between the variable substitution and 'is not computed'. Also changes double quotes to single quotes around the variable. The dropped word 'accuracy' is load-bearing — it specifies WHAT about the headline is uncomputed. Without it, the sentence reads as 'the headline string is not computed' rather than 'the headline accuracy is not computed.' Context (next sentence: 'It is a static string in the HTML source…') restores the meaning, but the punchy first sentence loses some force.

clause_by_clause.original: It is a static string in the HTML source code — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count.
clause_by_clause.v2: It is a static string in the HTML source — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count.
clause_by_clause.v2_audit_label: minor wording polish ('HTML source code' → 'HTML source')
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Drops 'code' from 'HTML source code'. Audit explicitly notes this. No semantic loss.

clause_by_clause.original: No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number.
clause_by_clause.v2: No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number;
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Period replaced with semicolon to merge with next clause. Verbatim wording. Acceptable.

clause_by_clause.original: The arithmetic is stated on the wins page, but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry.
clause_by_clause.v2: the arithmetic is stated on the wins page but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry.
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Comma dropped before 'but'; capital T → lowercase t (post-semicolon). Acceptable.

clause_by_clause.original: The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.
clause_by_clause.v2: The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.

clause_by_clause.original: When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns ${accuracyVariantList} — depending on which data source and counting method is used.
clause_by_clause.v2: Internal queries return ${accuracyVariantList} depending on which data source and counting method is used;
clause_by_clause.v2_audit_label: minor wording polish, full factual substrate preserved
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Condensed framing 'When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns' → 'Internal queries return'. Em-dash → space. Substantive content preserved.

clause_by_clause.original: The denominator is chosen to include only N acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries.
clause_by_clause.v2: the dome's public denominator includes only N acknowledged falsifications and excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries.
clause_by_clause.v2_audit_label: preserved (audit lists below-detection-threshold and acknowledged falsifications as preserved)
clause_by_clause.actual_diff: Drops 'is chosen to' (which carried a deliberate-selection implication). Adds 'the dome's public' qualifier. Net effect: slight softening of the editorial 'chosen' framing. The 'self-serving denominator' sentence elsewhere preserves the criticism, so net critique survives. Audit doesn't itemize this drop.
clause_by_clause.severity: minor (the 'is chosen to' framing carried real editorial weight)

holes_found.description:
Line-number documentation inaccuracy in change_3.where. v2 states 'currently line 1385 closing ' for the sc-hero scorecard's closing tag, but the sc-hero's closing is actually at the start of line 1386, sharing the line with the opening of the bordered accent box ('
'). Line 1385 is the closing
of the inner Timestamp Error sc-card. The structural anchor v2 describes (after sc-hero close, before bordered box) is unambiguous from context, but the line number is one off. Since change_2.where correctly states the bordered box is at lines 1386–1388, change_3 implicitly contradicts itself (it cites line 1385 as sc-hero close but lines 1386–1388 as the box that immediately follows — these can't both be true).
holes_found.recommendation: Update change_3.where to either (a) cite line 1386 as the sc-hero closing location, or (b) describe the position structurally without a line number ('immediately after the closing of the .scorecard.sc-hero element, which currently sits at the start of line 1386 sharing a line with the bordered box opening').

holes_found.description: Lead-clause word loss not itemized in content_preservation_audit. The original bordered-box prose opens with 'The headline "X" accuracy is not computed by any script in the model's repository.' v2's section opens with 'The headline 'X' is not computed by any script in the model's repository.' The word 'accuracy' is dropped — and it is load-bearing. Without 'accuracy,' the sentence reads as 'the headline string is not computed' rather than 'the headline accuracy is not computed.' Context (next sentence) restores meaning, but the punchy first sentence loses force. The audit's preserved_source_code_critique_clauses item 1 labels this 'near-verbatim' without itemizing the drop, which v1 review's H2 finding asked to itemize for transparency.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) restore 'accuracy' in the lead clause: 'The headline \${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy} accuracy is not computed by any script…' (matches the original verbatim), or (b) itemize this drop explicitly in content_preservation_audit alongside the existing 'HTML source code → HTML source' itemization, so the audit's 'near-verbatim' framing has the full diff itemized rather than the diff hidden behind aggregate framing.

holes_found.description: AC8/9/10 viewport-fold thresholds use 'top <= viewport_height' as the strict assertion (e.g., AC8: heading top <= 1080 at 1920×1080). This admits the boundary case where the heading's top edge sits at y = viewport_height — meaning the heading begins at the bottom edge of the viewport and is visually cut off. The intent is clearly 'fully above-fold' (the operator finding #3 was about reachability without scrolling), but the literal threshold leaves a small gap between 'technically passes' and 'actually visible.'
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten AC8/9/10 to either (a) 'heading top + heading rendered height <= viewport_height' (fully above-fold), or (b) add a buffer like 'heading top <= viewport_height - 80' to ensure the heading is meaningfully visible, not just 'first pixel at fold edge.' Both options remain testable. Not blocking — the looser current threshold is still measurable and forces a known-good measurement; this is precision-tightening, not a structural defect.

holes_found.description:
AC18 structural-order list omits the

Verdict Categories Used in This Review

element on line 1396, which sits between 'Downloads heading' (line 1390) and 'verdict-legend' (line 1397) in the actual Overview tab. AC18 jumps from 'Downloads heading' directly to 'verdict-legend' — non-essential omission, but a complete structural assertion should include the intervening h1.
holes_found.recommendation: Update AC18 to: 'title-block → verdict-bar-chart (EXP-209) → sc-hero scorecard → accuracy-math-section (NEW) → Downloads heading → Verdict Categories h1 → verdict-legend → breaking-news (Latest Findings) → nav.toc.' Alternatively, scope AC18 to 'these elements appear in this relative order' rather than 'this is the complete list.'

holes_found.description: Phrasing-consistency residue from H2-fix. v2 added a top-level preservation_framing_note that downgrades the audit to 'near-verbatim,' but individual clause descriptions still use mixed terminology — 'near-verbatim,' 'verbatim,' 'verbatim modulo punctuation,' 'VERBATIM.' Each per-clause use is locally accurate (the operator-mandated sentence really IS verbatim; the No-Python-script clause really IS verbatim modulo punctuation), so this is not a contradiction. But a fully-applied H2 fix would either harmonize the per-clause language or add a brief inline note where 'verbatim' is used (e.g., 'verbatim — operator-mandated' for the self-serving denominator clause).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish — add a one-line note to each per-clause 'verbatim' use specifying why it's verbatim there but elsewhere is 'near-verbatim.' Not blocking.

holes_found.description: Test-infrastructure gap for AC8/9/10 (Playwright reachability) and AC12 (Playwright accessibility-tree assertion). Currently test.js has no Playwright integration — its require()s are limited to fs and path. The Playwright dependency is in package.json (^1.59.1) and is used by build-scripts/generate-pdf.js, so the dependency is available. v2's notes_for_human_review_group[2] flags this and offers a downgrade path to static-layout assertions. This is not a content defect — the spec is correct — but the integration plan needs to specify HOW AC8/9/10/12 will actually be measured: (a) extend test.js with new Playwright assertions (small but real CI infrastructure work), or (b) defer to manual measurement at integration time, or (c) downgrade to static-layout assertions.
holes_found.recommendation: Before the decider integrates v2, the integration plan must include an explicit decision on the AC8/9/10/12 measurement pathway. If (a), the new Playwright assertions need authoring effort that's not currently in v2's content_diff (it only edits build-scripts/generate-html.js). If (b), document the integration-time measurement procedure and store evidence (DOMrect values per viewport size) in monitor/integrity/ or a comparable audit trail. If (c), update AC8/9/10/12 wording in v2 to reflect the static-layout fallback. Without this decision, AC8/9/10/12 are aspirational acceptance criteria — they pass at spec review but cannot be verified at integration.

stronger_arguments.description: v2's wording change in change_3 from the original 'Honest accuracy: 89.6%, not 94.5%' to 'Honest accuracy 89.6%, not the headline 94.5%' (adding 'the headline' qualifier) is a small clarity gain — it makes the contrast target explicit (the dome's HEADLINE number, not some generic 94.5%). preserved_load_bearing_content doesn't call this out as an improvement, only as preserved. Consider acknowledging this small clarity gain in the audit.

The defensive ternary in change_3.new_section_html is now the source-of-truth integration target (per v1 H1 fix). However, change_3.defensive_rendering_note also documents the same ternary separately. To prevent a future maintainer from removing the ternary thinking 'this is just a spec-section explanation, the actual code shouldn't have it,' consider adding an inline source comment to the new_section_html itself (e.g., '<!-- ams-variant is conditionally rendered when accuracyVariantList is non-empty; do not remove the ${...} ternary -->') so the rationale rides with the code, not just with the spec doc.

kernel_of_truth.description: v2 successfully addresses all four curmudgeon H1–H4 findings from v1 and all three operator findings from HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001. The element change to <section role="region" aria-labelledby> + visible <h3> is the right semantic fix for finding #2, and the new heading-navigation pathway it adds is a genuine a11y improvement over both v1's <aside> and the original bordered box (which had no heading at all). The AC8/9/10 measured-not-narrated reachability assertions are the right structural fix for finding #3 — even with the test-infrastructure gap (H6), the spec now has measurable thresholds, which is the operator's stated bar. The AC14 contrast measurements are independently verified to two decimal places against tokens read directly from generate-html.js source. The 'self-serving denominator' sentence is preserved verbatim per the operator override. The proposal package is conservative, well-documented, and ready for integration under the conditional approval rubric — with six minor documentation/spec-tightening recommendations from this re-review.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is a project-internal proposal review, not a dome-model-claim review. The 'kernel' here is acknowledging that v2 is substantively sound and the conditional rubric ('no new ESCALATE findings') is met.

conditional_approval_assessment.rubric: Per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 and HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001 step 4: clean curmudgeon re-review (no new ESCALATE findings) → automatic integration without a fresh operator HNOTE.
conditional_approval_assessment.integration_recommendation: PROCEED under the conditional approval pathway. None of the six findings rise to ESCALATE. The single moderate finding (H6, test-infrastructure gap) requires an integration-plan decision but does not block the spec itself. The five minor findings (H1 line-number, H2 lead-clause word loss, H3 threshold strictness, H4 AC18 omission, H5 phrasing residue) are documentation-quality improvements — the decider may apply them inline at integration time without re-routing for another review cycle, or defer them to a follow-up polish EXP. The conditional rubric explicitly accepts existing FIX/CLOSE-category findings.
conditional_approval_assessment.audit_trail: v1 review (queue_id 240) recommended minor_edit with H1–H4. v2 addresses all four. v2's own conditional rubric requires 'no new ESCALATE.' This re-review (queue_id 260) finds zero ESCALATE / zero critical / zero major findings. Integration may proceed.

text_fingerprint.note: Not applicable — proposal review, not a WIN/section text review. The reviewed file (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211-redesign-v2.json) is a spec document, not user-visible prose.

citations_verified: AC14 contrast ratios (--ink-2 dark on --bg dark = 8.33:1; --ink-2 light on --bg light = 12.16:1; --ink-3 dark on --bg dark = 4.71:1; --ink-3 light on --bg light = 5.59:1) — recomputed via WCAG 2.1 relative-luminance formula, all four match exactly to two decimals.
Token values for --ink-2/--ink-3/--bg in light, dark, and print themes — read directly from build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 496, 497, 645; match AC14 stated values.
Line 1379 hero sublabel for 'Actual Failures' — verified.
v1 review file existence and queue_id (queue_id 240) — verified.

HNOTE references (HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001, HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001, HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001, HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001) — referenced by name in v2; not independently re-read this run, but the v2 spec's per-HNOTE summaries are internally consistent.
citations_failed: v2 change_3.where attribution of line 1385 to sc-hero closing — actual line is 1386 (see H1).
citations_unchecked: ISS-1425 closure note text in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json — referenced by v2 but not re-read this run; v2's summary appears internally consistent.

EXP-202 token definitions (sourced from a different EXP file, not generate-html.js); v2 cites them but the canonical source was not opened — token values were verified against generate-html.js where they're applied, which is the load-bearing surface for AC14.
EXP-207, EXP-209, EXP-210, EXP-212 integration status — not independently confirmed against status.json; v2's claim that A1 is integrated is taken on its own report.

deeper_analysis: The most interesting question this v2 re-review raises is how to weigh the test-infrastructure gap (H6) under the conditional-approval rubric. The rubric says 'no new ESCALATE findings' → automatic integration. H6 is moderate, not ESCALATE. So integration may proceed. But H6 means AC8/9/10/12 are aspirational unless someone takes one of the three pathways listed. The most likely path is (b) — manual measurement at integration time — given that test.js has historically been kept lightweight and the team hasn't authored Playwright assertions yet. If the decider goes that route, the integration commit should include the measured DOMrect values as evidence (e.g., a JSON file in monitor/integrity/ or a commit-message block). Without that evidence, AC8/9/10/12 collapse from 'measured' back to 'narrative' — exactly the failure mode operator finding #3 was trying to fix. So the conditional rubric is met, but the integration plan needs a measurement-evidence requirement attached. Recommendation to decider: when popping queue_id 260, add a follow-up note to the integration step: 'Capture DOMrect values for .accuracy-math-section .ams-heading at 1920×1080, 2560×1440, and 390×844 viewports; record in monitor/integrity/ as supporting evidence for AC8/9/10. If values exceed thresholds, hold integration and route back to analyst.' This converts the test-infrastructure gap from a silent risk to a documented checkpoint without requiring new test.js code.

trigger_context.queue_reason: EXP-211-redesign v2 redraft complete. Re-review required before integration per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 conditional approval. v2 closes all four curmudgeon holes (H1-H4) plus operator FINDING #2/#3/additional. Clean re-review (no new ESCALATE) triggers auto-integration.
